purpose: Provides multi-dimensional similarity assessment across historical episodes rather than relying on any single analogue, acknowledging structural novelties that limit all analogues to <7.0/10 similarity
components: Seven-dimension scoring matrix for historical episode comparison
Structural novelty identification: mortgage lock-in (85%+ below 5%), >90% covenant-lite, AI investment cycle, simultaneous deglobalization + fiscal expansion at 6-7% GDP
Composite scoring weighted by dimension relevance
Explicit treatment of non-matching features as individually material
current_assessment: No single analogue scores above 7.0/10. The 1966-69 analogue downgraded from 6.5-7.0 to 5-6/10 after accounting for five material non-matches. Compound approach judged stronger than single-analogue reliance by pair_0 debate.
limitations: Dimension weights are subjective, creating an illusion of objectivity
Outcome distributions may be wider than any analogue framework suggests
At the point of compound structural novelty, analogue-based reasoning may be approaching unreliability
Does not produce actionable timing signals
